general.prompt: What did the U.S. just slap on most Brazilian goods, making Brazilian coffee even more expensive?
options: A 50% tariff on soccer balls
A 25% tariff
A 12.5% tariff on coffee only
A 25% tariff on Brazilian goods
general.summary: The U.S. imposed a 25% tariff on most Brazilian goods due to 'unfair trade practices,' with a possible additional 12.5% duty.

general.prompt: How did Argentina, led by Messi, secure their spot in the World Cup final?
options: Won on a penalty shootout after extra time
Messi scored a hat trick in the first half
Scored twice late in the match
Scored twice late in the match
general.summary: Argentina scored twice after the 85th minute to beat England and advance to the final against Spain.

general.prompt: What happened to New Zealand actor Sam Neill, according to his agent?
options: He died from pneumonia
He was abducted by aliens
He died from pneumonia
He retired from acting due to health issues
general.summary: Sam Neill died from pneumonia in Sydney, with his family calling his death 'sudden and unexpected.'

general.prompt: Why did police round up Hong Kong booksellers, according to the article?
options: As part of a widening crackdown on free speech
As part of a widening crackdown on free speech
For selling illegal Harry Potter knockoffs
Due to a tax evasion scandal
general.summary: Police rounded up booksellers as part of a widening crackdown on free speech since the 2019 protests.

general.prompt: What did Trump do to the new court-appointed top prosecutor in Seattle?
options: Gave him a promotion to a federal judgeship
Fired him immediately
Appointed him to a special task force
Fired him immediately
general.summary: Trump immediately fired the court-appointed top prosecutor in Seattle.

tech.prompt: Square Enix just dropped a new trailer for Final Fantasy Resonance. What's the art style called that makes it look like a retro dream?
options: Pixel
Resonance
Chibi
Voxel
tech.summary: Square Enix released a 'Pixel' trailer for Final Fantasy Resonance showcasing its retro-inspired art style.

tech.prompt: Langrisser: Sea of Sword is the new tactical RPG that just dropped. What's the key feature of its gameplay?
options: Underwater base building
Mario-style platforming
Real-time sword fighting
Turn-based strategy
tech.summary: Langrisser: Sea of Sword is a fantasy tactical RPG announced with a debut trailer.

tech.prompt: Mario Tennis Fever got a massive update. What's the new court called that's out of this world?
options: Rainbow Road
Galaxy Court
Black Hole Racket
Cloud Nine Court
tech.summary: Mario Tennis Fever version 1.1.0 added Galaxy Court, Black Hole Racket, and balance adjustments.

tech.prompt: AirPods finally got a feature people have been begging for. What is it?
options: Spatial audio for video calls
Wireless charging
Noise cancellation toggle
Custom EQ
tech.summary: AirPods public beta added custom EQ, a long-requested feature.

tech.prompt: Skullcandy's new headphones borrowed tech from Bose. How many features did they swipe?
options: Three
Five
All of them
None, it's all original
tech.summary: Skullcandy Crusher 1080 ANC includes three technologies never before seen in non-Bose headphones.

business.prompt: Why did South Korean stocks take a nosedive? Was the Bank of Korea's new boss trying to play hardball with inflation?
options: Investors were spooked by a sudden shortage of K-pop idols
China grew at slowest pace in 3 years
They ran out of kimchi and panic selling ensued
First rate hike in 3 years to fight inflation
business.summary: South Korea's stock market fell after the Bank of Korea raised interest rates for the first time in three years to combat inflation and currency weakness.

business.prompt: An ex-Fed advisor got over three years in the slammer. What did he lie about that got him in such hot water?
options: Lying about ties to China
Saying the Fed's interest rates were 'kinda spicy'
Falsifying his bank credit line ahead of an IPO
Pretending he was a secret agent for the K-pop industry
business.summary: A former Federal Reserve advisor was sentenced to over three years in prison for lying about his ties to China, in a prominent case targeting Chinese economic espionage.

business.prompt: China's economy grew at its slowest pace in over three years last quarter. What was the annualized growth rate that had everyone yawning?
options: 3.14% because they ran out of pi
6 billion dollars in added fuel costs
5.1% - wait, that's too fast, better make it slower
business.summary: China's economy grew at a 4.3% annualized pace in the last quarter, its slowest since late 2022, missing the strong 5% start to the year.

business.prompt: United Airlines expects a sky-high bill for fuel this year. How many billions are they forking over?
options: $6 billion
3 years in prison for lying
Enough to buy a small island made of avocados
All the gold from Fort Knox, but in jet fuel
business.summary: United Airlines anticipates $6 billion in additional fuel costs this year due to higher jet fuel prices, according to the article.

business.prompt: Anthropic is in talks to add billions to their credit line. What's the big event they're preparing for?
options: Rate hike in South Korea
Buying a private island to train their AI in seclusion
A massive pizza party for all AI researchers
An initial public offering (IPO)
business.summary: Anthropic is negotiating for billions in bank credit lines ahead of a planned initial public offering (IPO) this year.

sports.prompt: Argentina was minutes from elimination when they pulled off a miracle. Who scored twice late to send them to the World Cup final?
options: A mysterious goat that wandered onto the pitch
Lionel Messi
Thomas Tuchel’s tactical adjustments
A backup goalkeeper who snuck into the box
sports.summary: Led by Lionel Messi, Argentina scored twice after the 85th minute to beat England and advance to the final against Spain.

sports.prompt: The Sonics might be returning to Seattle, but what three issues did the NBA commissioner raise?
options: Whether to use a talking parrot as the new mascot
Whether Eileen Gu’s red carpet outfit was distracting
Expansion logistics, team ownership, and arena plans
Lack of enough giraffe mascots in the league
sports.summary: NBA commissioner Adam Silver discussed three issues regarding expansion and the potential return of the Seattle SuperSonics.

sports.prompt: The 2026 ESPYs red carpet was packed with stars. Which athlete was NOT seen there?
options: A golfer from the British Open
A fully grown penguin in a tuxedo
A robot that won an Olympic medal
Simone Biles
sports.summary: Eileen Gu, Simone Biles, and Alysa Liu were among athletes who walked the red carpet at the 2026 ESPYs.

sports.prompt: The 154th British Open is underway. When does the final round finish?
options: Right after the ESPYs red carpet
Sunday
Next Tuesday during a lunar eclipse
Thursday, after a brief intermission for tea
sports.summary: The 154th Open Championship is a golf tournament that runs through Sunday.

sports.prompt: England’s World Cup exit was blamed on many things. What was NOT the biggest problem, according to Sports Illustrated?
options: Argentina scoring twice late in the match
Thomas Tuchel’s changes out of fear
A group of rogue clowns distracting the team
Lack of proper pre-match snack selection
sports.summary: Thomas Tuchel's fearful changes were not the main issue for England's World Cup failure, but he still couldn't find the solution.

science.prompt: What cosmic rock star crashed into a New Jersey home, bringing a taste of ancient water?
options: A chunk of T. rex fossil that fell from space
A piece of a comet that smelled like naked mole-rat
A rare primitive meteorite with evidence of ancient water
A fragment from a nearby uninhabitable planet
science.summary: A meteorite that hit a New Jersey home in 2024 is a rare primitive meteorite with evidence of ancient water.

science.prompt: After 37 years of silence, what daring feat did engineers pull off with Voyager's thrusters?
options: Used a naked mole-rat queen's scent to lubricate them
Fixed a meteorite damage on the spacecraft
Successfully fired frozen thrusters after 37 years
Replaced the thrusters with ones from a T. rex hatchling
science.summary: Engineers successfully fired thrusters on Voyager that had been frozen and unused for 37 years.

science.prompt: How do naked mole-rat queens keep their subjects from staging a coup?
options: By releasing a chemical scent that alters hormone production
By using Voyager's thrusters as a warning
By threatening to throw meteorites at them
By running faster than T. rex babies
science.summary: The queen's unique scent alters hormone production in other females, preventing them from reproducing.

science.prompt: What did astronomers discover about planets near Barnard's Star?
options: They are perfect for T. rex vacation homes
They are made of naked mole-rat cheese
They contain ancient water like the New Jersey meteorite
They are small, strange, and utterly uninhabitable
science.summary: The planets orbiting Barnard's Star are small, strange, and completely uninhabitable.

science.prompt: What surprising skill did baby T. rexes have that would make any parent jealous?
options: They could dodge meteorites from New Jersey
They could fire thrusters like Voyager
They were born ready to run and feed themselves
They could sniff out naked mole-rat queens
science.summary: T. rex hatchlings were born ready to run and feed themselves.
